info.name: Car Rental API
info.description:
API collection for the Car Rental project. Import this file in Postman to test all endpoints.

## Authentication (when you need a token)

| Endpoint pattern | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` |
| --- | --- |
| `POST /api/login` | Public. Response includes `data.token`; Postman saves it to `{{token}}`. |
| `POST /api/v1/users` | Public registration route; the server may still return **403** if registration is disabled. |
| `GET /api/user`, `PUT /api/password`, `POST /api/logout` | Bearer token required. |
| All other `/api/v1/...` routes | Bearer token required. |

This collection sets collection-level Bearer auth with `{{token}}`. Public requests override auth to `No Auth`.

## Setup
1. Set `base_url` (default: `http://localhost`; add a port if needed, for example `http://localhost:8000`).
2. Run **Login**. The token is stored automatically. Seeded accounts after `php artisan migrate --seed`: `test@example.com` / `password` and `admin@example.com` / `password`.
3. Reuse saved variables from earlier responses: `car_id`, `driver_id`, `customer_id`, `transaction_id`, `booking_id`, `trip_report_id`, `car_import_id`, `driver_import_id`, `bill_payment_id`.

## Notes
- User registration requires `name`, `email`, `password`, and `password_confirmation`. Optional `role` defaults to `user`; public registration rejects `admin` with **422**.
- Change password requires `current_password`, `password`, and `password_confirmation`.
- Car import CSV columns: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number`.
- Driver import CSV columns: `first_name`, `last_name`, `license_number`, `license_expiry_date`, `address`, `phone_number`, `email`, `password`.
- Customers support hierarchy and contact fields: `parent_id`, `contact_person`, `contact_mobile_number`, `contact_email`.
- `GET /api/v1/bookings` uses global statuses `completed`, `today`, `ongoing`, `incoming`. `GET /api/v1/transactions/{id}/bookings` uses `upcoming` and `previous`, plus `period`, `car_id`, and `driver_id` filters.
- Bill numbers are generated automatically. Bill payment uploads use `multipart/form-data`.
- Trip reports are limited to admin users or the driver assigned to the booking. Snapshot fields are captured on create and do not change later.

request.description:
Public endpoint - **do not** send `Authorization`. Send JSON `email` and `password`. On success, use `data.token` as `Bearer` for all `/api/v1/*` routes (except register). Response includes `data.role` (`admin` or `user`) and `data.user.attributes.role` with the same stored value. Linked driver users still return `role: user` — driver access is determined by the linked driver profile, not this field.

Default seeded credentials: `test@example.com` / `password` (user), `admin@example.com` / `password` (admin).
**Bearer required.** Revokes the current Sanctum token. After this, set a new token via Login.

**Bearer required.** Returns the authenticated user resource for the current token. Read `data.attributes.role` for the stored authorization role (`admin` or `user`). Linked driver users still return `role: user`.

**Bearer required.** Updates the authenticated user's password.

**Required:** `current_password`, `password` (min 8), `password_confirmation`.

The new password must differ from `current_password`. Returns **422** when the current password is wrong, confirmation does not match, or validation fails.

Register a new user account. **No Bearer token** — this route is public.

**Required:** `name`, `email`, `password` (min 8), `password_confirmation`.

**Optional:** `role` — defaults to `user` when omitted. Public registration only accepts `user`; sending `admin` returns **422**.

Returns **403** when registration is disabled server-side (`allow_user_registration`). On success, response includes `data.attributes.role` (`admin` or `user`).

**Bearer required.** Create a car. Required body fields: `type`, `door` (integer), `seats` (integer), `year` (integer), `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number` (unique). Response attributes use camelCase (`plateNumber`, etc.).

Queue a CSV import of cars (multipart/form-data, `file` required).

**CSV header row (required):**
`type,door,seats,year,color,make,model,plate_number`

**Example row:**
`Sedan,4,5,2022,Black,Toyota,Camry,ABC-1234`

`door` and `seats` must be integers. `plate_number` must be unique across the database and within the file.
Fetch status/result summary for a queued car import job.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. Filter by vehicle type (string).
Optional. Filter by door count (integer, min 1).
Optional. Filter by seat count (integer, min 1).
Optional. Filter by year (integer).
Optional. Filter by color (string).
Optional. Filter by make (string).
Optional. Filter by model (string).
Optional. Filter by plate number (string).

request.description: **Bearer required.** Lists available cars. All query keys are optional; omit them to list with defaults. Response attributes: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plateNumber`.
**Bearer required.** Returns one car. Attributes: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plateNumber`, `createdAt`.
**Bearer required.** Partial update supported (`sometimes` rules). All fields optional: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number` (unique except current car).

**Bearer required.** Create a driver and automatically provision a linked user account. Required: `first_name`, `last_name`, `license_number` (unique), `license_expiry_date`, `address`, `phone_number`, `email` (unique user email), `password` (string, min 8). The new user is named `{first_name} {last_name}` with role `user`. Response includes `userId` for the auto-created account.

Queue a CSV import of drivers (multipart/form-data, `file` required). Each valid row creates a linked user account (same as `POST /api/v1/drivers`).

**CSV header row (required):**
`first_name,last_name,license_number,license_expiry_date,address,phone_number,email,password`

**Example row:**
`John,Doe,DL-12345678,2027-12-31,123 Main St,+1234567890,john.doe@example.com,password123`

`license_number` must be unique across the database and within the file. `email` must be unique across users and within the file. `password` must be at least 8 characters.
Fetch status/result summary for a queued driver import job.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. Search string (max 100) applied to driver listing.
Optional only if `end_date` is also omitted. If you use availability mode, **both** `start_date` and `end_date` are required (`end_date` must be on or after `start_date`).
Optional only if `start_date` is also omitted. **Required together with `start_date`** for drivers available in that period.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Default: paginated all drivers. **Date range mode:** when both `start_date` and `end_date` are sent, returns drivers available in that window (validated as a pair).
**Bearer required.** Returns one driver. Attributes: `firstName`, `lastName`, `licenseNumber`, `licenseExpiryDate`, `address`, `phoneNumber`, `userId` (null if not linked to a user account).

**Bearer required.** Partial update (`sometimes` rules). All fields optional: `first_name`, `last_name`, `license_number` (unique except current driver), `license_expiry_date`, `address`, `phone_number`. **Authorization:** admin users can update any driver; a non-admin user linked to this driver (via `user_id`) can update their own record. All others receive 403. **Admin-only field:** `user_id` (nullable integer) — links or unlinks the driver to a user account; silently ignored for non-admin callers.

item.name: Billing & bills
item.description: All routes here require **Bearer** auth. Query keys below are optional unless noted.

query.description: Optional. UUID of a customer; scopes summary to that customer.
Optional. Limits `totalUnpaid` to bills issued on or before this date.
Optional. Use with filter[paid_at][to]; limits `totalPaid` to bills paid on or after this date. `paid_at` to must be on or after from.
Optional. Limits `totalPaid` to bills paid on or before this date.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Aggregated billing for the authenticated user. Response `data.attributes`: `totalPaid` (integer, sum of amounts on `paid` bills) and `totalUnpaid` (integer, outstanding balance on `issued` and `partially_paid` bills). All `filter[...]` query keys are optional; omit `filter` entirely for an unfiltered summary.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. One of: issued_at, -issued_at, created_at, -created_at.
Optional. If set to `transaction` (comma-separated list allowed but only this token is valid), response adds `included` with related transactions.
Optional. Comma-separated: draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, cancelled.
Optional. Partial match against the bill invoice number, for example `INV-260500123`.
Optional. Use with filter[issued_at][to].
Optional. Must be on or after from when both set.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated bills for the current user across all customers. Response attributes include `billNumber`, `invoiceNumber`, `amount`, `amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, `status`, `issuedAt`, `dueAt`, `paidAt`. `amountPaid` is the sum of recorded payments; `remainingBalance` is `amount` minus `amountPaid` (never below 0). Supports partial invoice lookup with `filter[invoice_number]`.

**Bearer required.** Create a customer. Required: `name`, `type` (`personal` or `business`). Optional: `parent_id`, `contact_person`, `contact_mobile_number`, `contact_email`. `parent_id` must reference an existing customer or be omitted/null.

query.description: Optional: 1-100

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated customer list. Optional query: `per_page` (1-100). List responses include parent linkage when `parent_id` exists, but do not include parent attributes such as the parent name.

**Bearer required.** Returns one customer. Customer attributes include `name`, `type`, `parentId`, `contactPerson`, `contactMobileNumber`, and `contactEmail`. Single-resource responses may include parent attributes such as the parent name under `relationships.parent.data.attributes`.
**Bearer required.** Returns the immediate parent customer for `{{customer_id}}`. When the customer has no parent, the API returns **404** with `Customer has no parent account`.

body.raw:
{
    "name": "Iligan City Engineers Office Updated",
    "parent_id": null,
    "contact_person": "John Smith",
    "contact_mobile_number": "+639189876543",
    "contact_email": "john@example.com"
}

request.description: **Bearer required.** Update any subset of customer fields: `name`, `type`, `parent_id`, `contact_person`, `contact_mobile_number`, `contact_email`. Set `parent_id` to `null` to clear the parent. A customer cannot be its own parent or use one of its descendants as its parent.
**Bearer required.** Create a transaction for the customer in the URL. Body must include `name` (string, max 255).

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. `0`, `1`, `true`, or `false` — filter by whether the transaction has a bill.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated transactions for `{{customer_id}}`. Optional `has_bill` filters by bill presence.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. issued_at, -issued_at, created_at, -created_at.
Optional. Use `transaction` to populate JSON:API `included` with the bill's transaction.
Optional. Comma-separated bill statuses: draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, cancelled.
Optional. Partial match against the bill invoice number, for example `INV-260500321`.
Optional. Pair with filter[issued_at][to].
Optional. Must be on or after from when both are set.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Bills for `{{customer_id}}` belonging to the authenticated user. Same optional query keys and response attributes as **List bills (all)** (`amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, etc.), including partial `filter[invoice_number]` search, but scoped to this customer.
Requires `customer_id` (UUID) and `name` (string, max 255).
**Bearer required.** Requires `customer_id` (UUID) and `name` (string, max 255).

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. `0`, `1`, `true`, or `false` — filter by whether the transaction has a bill.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated transactions for the authenticated user. Optional `has_bill` filters by bill presence.

Returns transaction attributes including `name`, `userId`, `customerId`, and nested `bookings` (newest first) plus `included` booking details when present. Bookings in `included` follow the collection shape: car/driver attributes embedded, `relationships.transaction` linkage only (`type` + `id`).

**Bearer required.** Create a bill for a transaction. Only one bill per transaction. Body: required `amount` (integer, min 1); optional `notes`, `due_at`. Response includes auto-generated `billNumber` (`INV-YYYYMMDD-####`) and `invoiceNumber` (`INV-YYMM#####`), plus `amountPaid` (0 on create) and `remainingBalance` (equals `amount` on create).

**Bearer required.** Returns the bill for the transaction. Attributes include `billNumber`, `invoiceNumber`, `amount`, `amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, `status`, `notes`, `issuedAt`, `dueAt`, `paidAt`.
**Bearer required.** Returns an invoice-shaped payload for the transaction bill including `transaction`, optional `customer`, and `bookings` with embedded `car` and `driver` data.

response.name: 200 OK (example)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Partial update. Optional: `status` (`issued`, `paid`, `cancelled`), `amount`, `notes`, `due_at`. Manual status transitions: draft→issued/cancelled, issued→paid/cancelled. `partially_paid` is set automatically by the payment system. Bills in `paid` or `cancelled` status cannot be updated. `billNumber` and `invoiceNumber` are read-only.
Only draft bills can be deleted.

formdata.description: Required. Integer, min 1. Must not exceed the bill's remaining balance.
Required. One of: bank_transfer, cash, gcash.
Optional. Image file (jpg, jpeg, png, webp), max 10 MB.

request.description:
**Bearer required.** Record a payment (installment) against the transaction's bill. The request must be `multipart/form-data`.

**Required fields:** `amount` (integer, min 1 — must not exceed remaining balance), `method` (`bank_transfer` | `cash` | `gcash`), `reference_number` (string, max 255).

**Optional:** `notes`, `proof_image` (image: jpg/jpeg/png/webp, max 10 MB).

Only allowed when the bill status is `issued` or `partially_paid`. After recording, the bill status is recomputed automatically: partial payment → `partially_paid`; full payment → `paid`. Response attributes: `amount`, `method`, `referenceNumber`, `notes`, `proofImageUrl` (null when omitted), `paidAt`, `createdAt`.

**Bearer required.** Returns all payments recorded against the transaction's bill, ordered by `paid_at` descending. Response attributes per item: `amount`, `method`, `referenceNumber`, `notes`, `proofImageUrl`, `paidAt`, `createdAt`.
**Bearer required.** Deletes a payment record. After deletion, the bill status is recomputed automatically (e.g. `paid` may revert to `partially_paid` or `issued`). Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: Bookings
item.description: Booking resources expose `relationships.car`, `relationships.driver`, and `relationships.transaction`. List/collection endpoints include car and driver attributes plus transaction `id` only. Single-booking endpoints (`POST .../book`, `GET .../bookings/{id}`, `PUT .../bookings/{id}`) also return transaction attributes (`name`, `userId`, `customerId`, `createdAt`).

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional: completed, today, ongoing, or incoming

request.description:
**Bearer required.** Paginated bookings with the same response shape for admin and driver flows. Admin users receive bookings across their own transactions. Linked driver users receive only bookings assigned to their linked driver record. Ordered by `start_date` descending. Optional `status`: `completed` (end_date < now), `today` (start_date is today), `ongoing` (start_date <= now and end_date >= now), `incoming` (start_date > now).

Each item includes `relationships.car` and `relationships.driver` with embedded attributes. `relationships.transaction` contains `type` and `id` only — transaction attributes are omitted on collection responses.

query.description: Bookings where end_date is before now

request.description: **Bearer required.** Completed bookings in the authenticated scope. Admin scope = their transactions. Driver scope = bookings assigned to the linked driver.

query.description: Bookings whose start_date falls on today

request.description: **Bearer required.** Today bookings in the authenticated scope. Admin scope = their transactions. Driver scope = bookings assigned to the linked driver.

query.description: Bookings in progress (start_date <= now and end_date >= now)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Ongoing bookings in the authenticated scope. Admin scope = their transactions. Driver scope = bookings assigned to the linked driver.

query.description: Bookings that have not started yet (start_date > now)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Incoming bookings in the authenticated scope. Admin scope = their transactions. Driver scope = bookings assigned to the linked driver.

**Bearer required.** Create a booking. Required: `car_id`, `driver_id`, `price` (integer, min 0), `start_date`, `end_date` (≥ start_date). Optional: `note`.

Returns a single booking resource. `relationships.transaction.data` includes attributes: `name`, `userId`, `customerId`, `createdAt` (in addition to `type` and `id`).

query.description: Optional: 1-100
Optional: upcoming or previous
Optional: week or month
Optional: car UUID
Optional: driver UUID

request.description:
**Bearer required.** Paginated bookings for `{{transaction_id}}`. Ordered newest first (`created_at` desc, then `start_date` desc). Supported filters: `status` (`upcoming` or `previous`), `period` (`week` or `month`), `car_id`, `driver_id`.

Collection response: `relationships.car` and `relationships.driver` include attributes; `relationships.transaction` is linkage only (`type` + `id`, no attributes).

**Bearer required.** Returns one booking. Attributes: `note`, `price`, `startDate`, `endDate`. Relationships include embedded car/driver attributes and full transaction attributes (`name`, `userId`, `customerId`, `createdAt`) under `relationships.transaction.data`.

**Bearer required.** Partial update supported. All fields optional: `car_id`, `driver_id`, `price`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `note`. Returns the updated single booking resource with full `relationships.transaction.data.attributes` (`name`, `userId`, `customerId`, `createdAt`).

item.name: Trip Reports
item.description: Trip logs nested under a booking. **Authorization:** admin users or the driver assigned to the booking. Transaction owners who are not the assigned driver receive `403`.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Admin or assigned driver only. Required: `report_date`. Optional: `po_number`, `time_in`/`time_out` (`H:i`), `rate`, `odometer_in`/`odometer_out`, `fuel_liters`, `fuel_amount`, `invoice_or_or_number`, `collection_amount`, `percentage` (0–100), `destinations` (max 6 `{from, to}` entries). Response includes immutable snapshots (`driverNameSnapshot`, `carPlateNumberSnapshot`, `customerNameSnapshot`, `transactionNameSnapshot`, etc.).
**Bearer required.** Admin or assigned driver only. Returns all trip reports for the booking.
**Bearer required.** Admin or assigned driver only. Returns one trip report with snapshot attributes.
**Bearer required.** Admin or assigned driver only. Partial update (`sometimes` rules). Snapshots are not changed on update.
**Bearer required.** Admin or assigned driver only. Returns 204 No Content.

query.description: Required: 'car' or 'driver'
Required: Start date
Required: End date (must be after start)

request.description: **Bearer required.** `type`, `start`, and `end` are required query parameters.
